sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Peritoneal mesothelioma is characterized by recurrent mutations in tumor suppressor genes and epigenetic regulators, with a distinct molecular landscape compared to pleural mesothelioma [70].

sections.heading: BAP1
sections.content: The most frequently altered gene is BAP1 (BRCA1-associated protein 1), a tumor suppressor involved in multiple pathways including cell cycle regulation and RNA processing. Inactivation mechanisms include point mutations, copy number loss, structural rearrangements, and minute chromosomal deletions [62-64,67-69]. Aberrant BAP1 protein expression (loss of nuclear staining) is found in ~50%-70% of epithelioid peritoneal mesotheliomas but in <20% of sarcomatoid types [31-37,38].

sections.heading: Other commonly mutated genes
sections.content: Other commonly mutated genes include NF2, TP53, SETD2, and others [62-66].

sections.heading: Chromosomal abnormalities
sections.content: Chromosomal abnormalities are common, with loss of the 9p region containing CDKN2A and 22q containing NF2. Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A by FISH is present in ~60% of mesotheliomas overall, but the prevalence in peritoneal mesotheliomas is lower (8%-26%) [40-44,57-59]. Similarly, loss of MTAP (methylthioadenosine phosphorylase) by IHC, a surrogate for 9p loss, occurs in 14%-16% of peritoneal mesotheliomas [40-44]. Hemizygous loss of NF2 is found in ~50% of pleural mesotheliomas and is also relevant to peritoneal disease [61].

sections.heading: ALK rearrangements
sections.content: A rare subset of peritoneal mesotheliomas, particularly in younger patients, harbors oncogenic ALK rearrangements (e.g., STRN-ALK), which may confer sensitivity to ALK inhibitor therapy [49-51,53,54].

sections.heading: Germline mutations
sections.content: Germline mutations in cancer susceptibility genes, primarily BAP1 but also BRCA2, CDKN2A, VHL, and others, are present in 12%-16% of patients with pleural and peritoneal mesothelioma, predisposing to earlier onset and family history [48,72,73].

sections.heading: Genomic near-haploidization
sections.content: Additionally, rare genomic near-haploidization with extensive loss of heterozygosity has been described, sometimes associated with TP53 and/or SETDB1 mutations [62].
